title: addDocument not idempotent on duplicate symbolId (double-counts length & postings)

findingDetails.evidence: On a repeated symbolId, nextNumericId() returns the existing id, documents.set() overwrites, but totalDocumentLength += length runs again and postings.docIds/*Tfs.push() re-append under the same numericId, duplicating that doc's postings for each term.
findingDetails.why: Would corrupt averageLength and per-term scoring (a doc counted twice). Not reachable on the production path: querySymbolIndexRows selects from code_nodes where symbol_id is TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE (code-graph-db.ts:161), so the exported index/addDocument are only exposed to duplicates via direct API/test misuse.
findingDetails.recommendation: Guard addDocument against an already-present symbolId (early-return or rebuild that doc's postings) so the exported SymbolPackedBm25Index is safe for arbitrary callers, not only the dedup-guaranteed DB caller.
